# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: PR Target Build & Test

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read # Required for actions/checkout to read repository contents
      pull-requests: write # Required for actions/github-script to post comments on PRs

    steps:
      - name: Checkout PR merge commit
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        # For pull_request_target, actions/checkout@v4 by default checks out
        # the merge commit (PR head merged into base branch). This is the
        # recommended and safest way to test external contributions with
        # elevated GITHUB_TOKEN permissions, as it runs the code that would
        # actually be merged.

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '18' # Or your project's required Node.js version
          cache: 'npm' # Cache node modules for faster builds

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci # Or your project's dependency installation command (e.g., yarn install)

      - name: Run tests
        id: run_tests
        # Capture test output to a file and continue on error to ensure
        # the comment step always runs.
        run: |
          npm test 2>&1 | tee test_output.txt
        continue-on-error: true # Allow the workflow to continue even if tests fail

      - name: Post test results as comment
        uses: actions/github-script@v6
        if: always() # Ensure this step runs regardless of previous step outcomes
        with:
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs').promises;
            const testOutput = await fs.readFile('test_output.txt', 'utf8')
              .catch(() => 'No detailed test output file found.');

            const jobStatus = process.env.JOB_STATUS; // 'success', 'failure', 'cancelled', 'skipped'
            const icon = jobStatus === 'success' ? '✅' : '❌';
            const prNumber = context.issue.number;

            if (!prNumber) {
              console.log('Not a pull request context, skipping comment.');
              return;
            }

            const commentBody = `### ${icon} Build & Test Results (${jobStatus})\n\n` +
                              `\`\`\`\n${testOutput.substring(0, 65535)}\n\`\`\`\n\n` +
                              `[Workflow Run](${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }})`;

            await github.rest.issues.createComment({
              issue_number: prNumber,
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              body: commentBody
            });
        env:
          JOB_STATUS: ${{ job.status }}
